notes: Both disagreement entries name the axis substantively. The prajñā-vādān entry distinguishes the descriptive ('learned words') vs. ironic ('Arjuna's wisdom-talk is shaped like wisdom but contradicts itself' — Shankara) vs. category-of-bad-reasoning ('speculative concoction not according to Vedic injunction' — Madhva) readings, and explains why the disagreement is doctrinally substantive: modern translations flatten the rebuke that the commentarial tradition reads as primary. The paṇḍitāḥ entry distinguishes generic-wise vs. Shankara's etymological 'knowers of paṇḍā (Self-knowledge)' vs. Ramanuja's narrower 'those who have grasped body/self distinction' — and explains why this matters for whether the verse is making a general or technical claim. Neither entry collapses into 'the translators differ in tone'; both name what is at stake.
